text: Many websites handle sensitive information like passwords and credit card numbers. To protect this data, developers use several security methods. First, HTTPS encrypts the connection between your browser and the server, preventing others from reading the data. Next, authentication ensures only authorized users can access certain areas, often using passwords or two-factor verification. Additionally, input validation checks user-entered data for correct format and blocks harmful code. Without these precautions, common vulnerabilities such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting can allow attackers to steal information or take control of a site. Good security practices are essential for user trust and safety online.
